# Rule catalogue

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_rules_doc.py — do not edit by hand. -->

Rule IDs are a stable public contract: they never change meaning and are
never reused. Disable or re-rank any rule via `.cairn.yaml`
(see [configuration.md](configuration.md)).

| ID | Severity | Category | Title |
|----|----------|----------|-------|
| [AZ001](#az001) | HIGH | SECURITY | Network security group rule open to the internet |
| [AZ002](#az002) | HIGH | SECURITY | Storage account allows public blob access |
| [AZ003](#az003) | CRITICAL | SECURITY | SQL server allows public network access |
| [AZ004](#az004) | MEDIUM | SECURITY | Managed disk is not encrypted with a customer key |
| [AZ005](#az005) | MEDIUM | COST | Oversized virtual machine |
| [AZ006](#az006) | LOW | GOVERNANCE | Resource has no tags |
| [COST001](#cost001) | MEDIUM | COST | Oversized EC2 instance |
| [COST002](#cost002) | LOW | COST | gp2 volume — gp3 is cheaper and faster |
| [COST003](#cost003) | LOW | COST | Unassociated Elastic IP |
| [COST004](#cost004) | MEDIUM | COST | Oversized RDS instance class |
| [COST005](#cost005) | LOW | COST | Previous-generation instance type |
| [COST006](#cost006) | LOW | COST | EBS volume attached to nothing |
| [GCP001](#gcp001) | HIGH | SECURITY | Firewall rule open to the internet |
| [GCP002](#gcp002) | HIGH | SECURITY | Storage bucket is publicly accessible |
| [GCP003](#gcp003) | CRITICAL | SECURITY | Cloud SQL instance has a public IP |
| [GCP004](#gcp004) | MEDIUM | SECURITY | Compute instance without Shielded VM |
| [GCP005](#gcp005) | MEDIUM | COST | Oversized machine type |
| [GCP006](#gcp006) | LOW | GOVERNANCE | Resource has no labels |
| [GOV001](#gov001) | LOW | GOVERNANCE | Resource has no tags |
| [GOV002](#gov002) | MEDIUM | GOVERNANCE | Required tag missing (org policy) |
| [K8S001](#k8s001) | CRITICAL | SECURITY | Privileged container |
| [K8S002](#k8s002) | HIGH | SECURITY | Container may run as root |
| [K8S003](#k8s003) | HIGH | SECURITY | hostPath volume mounts the node filesystem |
| [K8S004](#k8s004) | MEDIUM | RELIABILITY | Unpinned image tag |
| [K8S005](#k8s005) | MEDIUM | COST | Container without resource limits |
| [K8S006](#k8s006) | LOW | RELIABILITY | Container without health probes |
| [REL001](#rel001) | MEDIUM | RELIABILITY | Database has no automated backups |
| [REL002](#rel002) | LOW | RELIABILITY | S3 bucket versioning disabled |
| [REL003](#rel003) | MEDIUM | RELIABILITY | Database can be deleted without protection |
| [SEC001](#sec001) | HIGH | SECURITY | Security group ingress open to the internet |
| [SEC002](#sec002) | HIGH | SECURITY | S3 bucket without server-side encryption |
| [SEC003](#sec003) | HIGH | SECURITY | S3 bucket with a public ACL |
| [SEC004](#sec004) | CRITICAL | SECURITY | Database instance is publicly accessible |
| [SEC005](#sec005) | HIGH | SECURITY | Database storage is not encrypted |
| [SEC006](#sec006) | HIGH | SECURITY | EBS volume is not encrypted |
| [SEC007](#sec007) | CRITICAL | SECURITY | IAM policy allows * on * |
| [SEC008](#sec008) | CRITICAL | SECURITY | Hardcoded secret in configuration |
| [SEC009](#sec009) | MEDIUM | SECURITY | EC2 instance does not require IMDSv2 |
| [SEC010](#sec010) | HIGH | SECURITY | Load balancer listener speaks plaintext HTTP |
| [VS001](#vs001) | MEDIUM | RELIABILITY | Virtual machine has no resource limits |
| [VS002](#vs002) | LOW | COST | Virtual disk is not thin-provisioned |
| [VS003](#vs003) | LOW | GOVERNANCE | VM has no annotation / owner |

## AZ001

**Network security group rule open to the internet** · HIGH · SECURITY

An inbound NSG rule allows any source (*/Internet). SSH (22) and RDP (3389) exposure is rated CRITICAL.

*Applies to:* `azurerm_network_security_rule`

*References:* https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview

## AZ002

**Storage account allows public blob access** · HIGH · SECURITY

allow_nested_items_to_be_public / allow_blob_public_access is enabled.

*Applies to:* `azurerm_storage_account`

*References:* https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/anonymous-read-access-prevent

## AZ003

**SQL server allows public network access** · CRITICAL · SECURITY

public_network_access_enabled is true, exposing the database endpoint.

*Applies to:* `azurerm_mssql_server`, `azurerm_postgresql_server`

*References:* https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-sql/database/network-access-controls-overview

## AZ004

**Managed disk is not encrypted with a customer key** · MEDIUM · SECURITY

No disk_encryption_set_id is set, so the disk uses only platform-managed keys.

*Applies to:* `azurerm_managed_disk`

*References:* https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/disk-encryption

## AZ005

**Oversized virtual machine** · MEDIUM · COST

The VM size is in a large SKU family and is likely over-provisioned.

*Applies to:* `azurerm_linux_virtual_machine`, `azurerm_windows_virtual_machine`

*References:* https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/sizes

## AZ006

**Resource has no tags** · LOW · GOVERNANCE

Untagged Azure resources break cost attribution and ownership.

*Applies to:* `azurerm_linux_virtual_machine`, `azurerm_windows_virtual_machine`, `azurerm_storage_account`, `azurerm_managed_disk`, `azurerm_mssql_server`

*References:* https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/tag-resources

## COST001

**Oversized EC2 instance** · MEDIUM · COST

The instance type is in a very large size class. Most workloads run well below 20% utilization; right-sizing is the highest-yield FinOps action.

*Applies to:* `aws_instance`

*References:* https://aws.amazon.com/aws-cost-management/aws-cost-optimization/right-sizing/

## COST002

**gp2 volume — gp3 is cheaper and faster** · LOW · COST

gp3 offers the same baseline performance as gp2 at ~20% lower GB-month price, with independently provisionable IOPS.

*Applies to:* `aws_ebs_volume`

*References:* https://aws.amazon.com/ebs/general-purpose/

## COST003

**Unassociated Elastic IP** · LOW · COST

An Elastic IP that is not attached to anything is billed hourly for doing nothing.

*Applies to:* `aws_eip`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/elastic-ip-addresses-eip.html

## COST004

**Oversized RDS instance class** · MEDIUM · COST

The database instance class is in a very large size class.

*Applies to:* `aws_db_instance`

*References:* https://aws.amazon.com/rds/instance-types/

## COST005

**Previous-generation instance type** · LOW · COST

Previous-generation families (t2/m4/c4/r4) cost more than their modern equivalents for less performance.

*Applies to:* `aws_instance`

*References:* https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/previous-generation/

## COST006

**EBS volume attached to nothing** · LOW · COST

A volume with no aws_volume_attachment referencing it is billed every month for storing data nothing reads.

*Applies to:* `aws_ebs_volume`

*References:* https://aws.amazon.com/ebs/pricing/

## GCP001

**Firewall rule open to the internet** · HIGH · SECURITY

A firewall rule allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0. SSH (22) and RDP (3389) exposure is rated CRITICAL.

*Applies to:* `google_compute_firewall`

*References:* https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/firewalls

## GCP002

**Storage bucket is publicly accessible** · HIGH · SECURITY

Uniform bucket-level access is disabled, allowing per-object public ACLs.

*Applies to:* `google_storage_bucket`

*References:* https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/uniform-bucket-level-access

## GCP003

**Cloud SQL instance has a public IP** · CRITICAL · SECURITY

ipv4_enabled is true, exposing the database on a public IP.

*Applies to:* `google_sql_database_instance`

*References:* https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/configure-private-ip

## GCP004

**Compute instance without Shielded VM** · MEDIUM · SECURITY

shielded_instance_config with secure boot is not enabled.

*Applies to:* `google_compute_instance`

*References:* https://cloud.google.com/compute/shielded-vm/docs/shielded-vm

## GCP005

**Oversized machine type** · MEDIUM · COST

The machine type is in a large class and is likely over-provisioned.

*Applies to:* `google_compute_instance`

*References:* https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/machine-resource

## GCP006

**Resource has no labels** · LOW · GOVERNANCE

Unlabeled GCP resources break cost attribution and ownership.

*Applies to:* `google_compute_instance`, `google_storage_bucket`, `google_sql_database_instance`

*References:* https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/creating-managing-labels

## GOV001

**Resource has no tags** · LOW · GOVERNANCE

Untagged resources cannot be attributed to a team, environment or cost center — FinOps and ownership both go blind.

*Applies to:* `aws_instance`, `aws_s3_bucket`, `aws_db_instance`, `aws_ebs_volume`, `aws_security_group`, `aws_eip`, `aws_launch_template`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/tagging-best-practices/tagging-best-practices.html

## GOV002

**Required tag missing (org policy)** · MEDIUM · GOVERNANCE

The organization's policy (required_tags in .cairn.yaml) mandates tags this resource does not carry. Inactive unless required_tags is configured.

*Applies to:* `aws_instance`, `aws_s3_bucket`, `aws_db_instance`, `aws_ebs_volume`, `aws_security_group`, `aws_eip`, `aws_launch_template`

## K8S001

**Privileged container** · CRITICAL · SECURITY

privileged: true removes container isolation entirely — the container is effectively root on the node.

*Applies to:* `k8s_pod`, `k8s_deployment`, `k8s_statefulset`, `k8s_daemonset`, `k8s_job`, `k8s_cronjob`, `k8s_replicaset`

*References:* https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/

## K8S002

**Container may run as root** · HIGH · SECURITY

Neither the pod nor the container enforces runAsNonRoot, so a compromised process starts with uid 0 in the container.

*Applies to:* `k8s_pod`, `k8s_deployment`, `k8s_statefulset`, `k8s_daemonset`, `k8s_job`, `k8s_cronjob`, `k8s_replicaset`

*References:* https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

## K8S003

**hostPath volume mounts the node filesystem** · HIGH · SECURITY

hostPath pierces the container boundary; with a writable mount it is a node-takeover primitive.

*Applies to:* `k8s_pod`, `k8s_deployment`, `k8s_statefulset`, `k8s_daemonset`, `k8s_job`, `k8s_cronjob`, `k8s_replicaset`

*References:* https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes/#hostpath

## K8S004

**Unpinned image tag** · MEDIUM · RELIABILITY

':latest' (or no tag) means every restart may pull different code — rollbacks and reproducibility break silently.

*Applies to:* `k8s_pod`, `k8s_deployment`, `k8s_statefulset`, `k8s_daemonset`, `k8s_job`, `k8s_cronjob`, `k8s_replicaset`

*References:* https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/

## K8S005

**Container without resource limits** · MEDIUM · COST

No limits means one runaway pod can starve the node (reliability) and the scheduler can't bin-pack (cost). Requests+limits are how Kubernetes capacity stays attributable.

*Applies to:* `k8s_pod`, `k8s_deployment`, `k8s_statefulset`, `k8s_daemonset`, `k8s_job`, `k8s_cronjob`, `k8s_replicaset`

*References:* https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

## K8S006

**Container without health probes** · LOW · RELIABILITY

Without liveness/readiness probes, Kubernetes routes traffic to dead processes and never restarts wedged ones.

*Applies to:* `k8s_deployment`, `k8s_statefulset`, `k8s_daemonset`

*References:* https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/

## REL001

**Database has no automated backups** · MEDIUM · RELIABILITY

backup_retention_period is 0 or unset, so point-in-time recovery is impossible: an accidental delete or corruption is permanent.

*Applies to:* `aws_db_instance`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_WorkingWithAutomatedBackups.html

## REL002

**S3 bucket versioning disabled** · LOW · RELIABILITY

Without versioning, overwritten or deleted objects are gone; with it, mistakes and ransomware-style overwrites are recoverable.

*Applies to:* `aws_s3_bucket`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/Versioning.html

## REL003

**Database can be deleted without protection** · MEDIUM · RELIABILITY

deletion_protection is not enabled, so one wrong terraform destroy or console click removes the database and its endpoint.

*Applies to:* `aws_db_instance`, `aws_rds_cluster`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_DeleteInstance.html

## SEC001

**Security group ingress open to the internet** · HIGH · SECURITY

An ingress rule allows traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0. Exposing services to the whole internet is the most common cloud breach vector; SSH/RDP exposure is rated CRITICAL.

*Applies to:* `aws_security_group`, `aws_security_group_rule`, `aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-security-groups.html · https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/amazon_web_services

## SEC002

**S3 bucket without server-side encryption** · HIGH · SECURITY

No server-side encryption is configured inline or via an aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration companion resource, so objects are stored unencrypted at rest.

*Applies to:* `aws_s3_bucket`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucket-encryption.html

## SEC003

**S3 bucket with a public ACL** · HIGH · SECURITY

The bucket ACL grants public read (or read/write) access.

*Applies to:* `aws_s3_bucket`, `aws_s3_bucket_acl`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-control-block-public-access.html

## SEC004

**Database instance is publicly accessible** · CRITICAL · SECURITY

publicly_accessible = true puts the database on the public internet.

*Applies to:* `aws_db_instance`, `aws_rds_cluster_instance`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_VPC.WorkingWithRDSInstanceinaVPC.html

## SEC005

**Database storage is not encrypted** · HIGH · SECURITY

storage_encrypted is false or unset, so data at rest is unencrypted.

*Applies to:* `aws_db_instance`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Overview.Encryption.html

## SEC006

**EBS volume is not encrypted** · HIGH · SECURITY

The EBS volume does not enable encryption at rest.

*Applies to:* `aws_ebs_volume`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-encryption.html

## SEC007

**IAM policy allows * on *** · CRITICAL · SECURITY

An IAM policy grants every action on every resource — full account admin. One leaked credential becomes a full compromise.

*Applies to:* `aws_iam_policy`, `aws_iam_role_policy`, `aws_iam_user_policy`, `aws_iam_group_policy`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html#grant-least-privilege

## SEC008

**Hardcoded secret in configuration** · CRITICAL · SECURITY

A credential-looking attribute holds a literal value. Anything committed to Git should be assumed leaked.

*References:* https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/configuration-language/sensitive-variables

## SEC009

**EC2 instance does not require IMDSv2** · MEDIUM · SECURITY

Without http_tokens = "required", the instance accepts IMDSv1 requests, enabling SSRF credential-stealing attacks.

*Applies to:* `aws_instance`, `aws_launch_template`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/configuring-instance-metadata-service.html

## SEC010

**Load balancer listener speaks plaintext HTTP** · HIGH · SECURITY

The listener accepts unencrypted HTTP. Credentials, cookies and session tokens crossing it are readable in transit.

*Applies to:* `aws_lb_listener`, `aws_alb_listener`

*References:* https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/create-https-listener.html

## VS001

**Virtual machine has no resource limits** · MEDIUM · RELIABILITY

No CPU/memory reservation or limit is set, so one noisy VM can starve the host and its neighbours.

*Applies to:* `vsphere_virtual_machine`

*References:* https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-vSphere/index.html

## VS002

**Virtual disk is not thin-provisioned** · LOW · COST

Eager/thick provisioning reserves the full disk up front. On shared datastores thin provisioning reclaims idle capacity.

*Applies to:* `vsphere_virtual_machine`

*References:* https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-vSphere/index.html

## VS003

**VM has no annotation / owner** · LOW · GOVERNANCE

No annotation is set, so ownership and purpose are untrackable.

*Applies to:* `vsphere_virtual_machine`

*References:* https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-vSphere/index.html
